All Classes and Interfaces

Class
Description
App
Entry point of the Cincuentazo application.
Card
Represents a single immutable playing card, defined by its suit and rank.
CardClickListener
Custom listener contract notified when the human player clicks on a card from their hand.
CardMouseAdapter
Adapter that bridges low-level JavaFX mouse events to the higher-level CardClickListener contract.
CardView
Visual representation of a single playing card in the user interface.
Deck
Represents the deck of cards used in a game of Cincuentazo.
EmptyDeckException
Checked exception thrown when an operation requires drawing a card but the deck is empty and cannot be refilled.
GameController
Controller for the main game view of Cincuentazo.
GameLogic
Central game engine that orchestrates a full game of Cincuentazo.
GameStateException
Unchecked exception thrown when an operation is attempted in an invalid game state — for example, trying to play a card before the game has been set up, or after it has already finished.
InvalidMoveException
Checked exception thrown when a player attempts to play a card that would break the core rule of Cincuentazo: making the table sum exceed 50.
MachineStrategy
Encapsulates the decision logic a machine player uses to choose which card to play on its turn.
MachineStrategy.Move
Represents a chosen move: which card to play and with what value.
MachineTurnService
Service that schedules the timed actions of a machine player using background threads, so the user interface never freezes.
Player
Represents a player in a game of Cincuentazo, either the human player or a machine opponent.
Rank
Represents the rank of a card and its associated value rules in the game of Cincuentazo.
StartController
Controller for the start view, where the human player chooses how many machine opponents (1, 2 or 3) to play against before starting the game.
Suit
Represents the four suits of a standard French-suited deck of cards.
Table
Represents the table at the center of a Cincuentazo game.
ViewManager
Centralized manager responsible for loading FXML views and swapping scenes on the primary stage.
